summary: Security hardening tests completed successfully. All 14/14 tests passed. Verified: 1) Admin login with correct credentials works, 2) Admin brute force protection locks out after 5 failed attempts, 3) Customer login works, 4) Customer brute force protection works, 5) MR login works, 6) MR brute force protection works, 7) OTP send rate limiting (5/min) works, 8) OTP verify brute force protection works, 9) JWT_SECRET is required (no weak fallback), 10) Customer/MR token expiry is 7 days.

minor.issue: CORS_ORIGINS is set to '*' in .env - consider locking down for production

action_items: Consider setting CORS_ORIGINS to specific allowed origins for production deployment
critical_code_review_comments: JWT_SECRET is properly required from .env with no weak fallback - VERIFIED
Customer token expiry reduced to 7 days (from 30 days) - VERIFIED
MR token expiry reduced to 7 days (from 30 days) - VERIFIED
Admin login brute force protection: 5 attempts → 15 min lockout - VERIFIED
Customer login brute force protection: 5 attempts → 15 min lockout - VERIFIED
MR login brute force protection: 5 attempts → 15 min lockout - VERIFIED
OTP verify brute force protection: 5 attempts → 15 min lockout - VERIFIED
Rate limiting uses _get_real_ip() to handle X-Forwarded-For headers - VERIFIED
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security hardening fully implemented and tested. Brute force protection uses in-memory tracking with IP:identifier keys. Lockout persists for 15 minutes. Use unique email/phone combos for brute force tests to avoid cross-contamination. JWT_SECRET is required in .env (no fallback). Customer/MR tokens expire in 7 days, admin tokens in 24 hours.
